contract_scope: One GGUF model artifact tracked by the model-inference backend registry. Metadata is persisted in SQLite; the artifact itself is stored in the King object store and identified by the flat object_store_key. This envelope is the request/response shape of GET /api/models/{model_id}, the array item shape of GET /api/models, and the body shape accepted by POST /api/models (with X-Model-* headers + raw binary body).
honesty_rules: model_id is deterministically generated server-side on POST; clients MUST NOT supply one. The pattern mdl-<16 lowercase hex chars> is required.

artifact.sha256_hex MUST equal the SHA-256 of the bytes stored in the object store (king integrity_sha256). The registry does NOT trust a client-supplied checksum; the value returned on create is computed from the actual persisted object.
artifact.byte_length MUST equal the content_length reported by king_object_store_get_metadata at create time. It is not backfilled from the HTTP Content-Length header.
artifact.object_store_key MUST be flat (no '/' separators); King rejects slash-containing object IDs.

On POST the server streams the body through king_object_store_put_from_stream; there is no in-memory copy of the full payload beyond the transport-layer body string that king_http1_server_listen_once already delivers.

License, source_url, family, parameter_count, and context_length come from the client headers or JSON body and are treated as untrusted metadata. They are NOT independently verified against the GGUF header in this leaf; a separate future leaf will add GGUF magic/metadata parsing.
Deletion removes the row AND issues king_object_store_delete; a failed object-store delete fails the whole deletion and the row remains.
